title: What supercomputing taught me about small hardware
preview: Running optimization experiments on TACC's cluster then writing firmware for a microcontroller changed how I think about computation fundamentally...

detail.body:
At TACC I had access to thousands of CPU cores and hundreds of GB of RAM. Running a 50-robot path-planning optimization took 3 seconds on the cluster. The same computation on a laptop took 4 minutes.

Then I went back to writing firmware for the ESP32-S3. 512 KB of RAM. 240 MHz. No OS scheduler. No malloc safety net.

The mindset shift is total. On HPC you optimize for throughput and correctness. On a microcontroller you optimize for worst-case latency and memory footprint. Every byte matters. Every millisecond of blocking matters.

The unexpected benefit: spending a summer thinking at HPC scale made me much better at MCU work. When you truly understand what computation costs, you make better decisions at every level of the stack.
